title: Can't resolve @angular-devkit/build-angular/node_modules/@babel/runtime/regenerator
author: SimoneMSR
body:
I use exceljs in a project that uses Angular13. When I run `ng build` I receive:

` Can't resolve 'dir/node_modules/@angular-devkit/build-angular/node_modules/@babel/runtime/regenerator' in 'dir\node_modules\exceljs\dist'`

in my `node_modules` folder I have
- `@angular-devkit/build-angular/node_modules` that has no `@babel` folder
- `@babel/runtime/generator` directly there

If I copy the `@babel` folder in the build-andular/node_modules, everything works.
I am asking if you can suggest another solution.

Please find attached my `package.json`
[package.txt](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/7526244/package.txt)

comments.author: edgarg42
comments.body:
hello, I ran into this same issue while doing the Angular13 upgrade and found this post. It seems that an explicit install of the `@babel/runtime` library is needed. Here is the post for cross reference, hope this helps.

https://github.com/FranckFreiburger/vue-pdf/issues/211

comments.author: Valentin331
comments.body:
> hello, I ran into this same issue while doing the Angular13 upgrade and found this post. It seems that an explicit install of the `@babel/runtime` library is needed. Here is the post for cross reference, hope this helps.
> 
> [FranckFreiburger/vue-pdf#211](https://github.com/FranckFreiburger/vue-pdf/issues/211)

This also worked for me, thank you!

comments.author: cybercussion
comments.body: I just ran into this on a project that was previously working but decided to nuke my node_modules folder now can't get it working again.  I guess we need to cd into the node_modules/@angular... to pull it off?
